reasoning: The informal statement says there are infinitely many primes p ≡ -1 (mod 4). The Lean statement `∀ N, ∃ p ≥ N, Nat.Prime p ∧ p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` expresses "infinitely many" in the standard way: for every natural number N, there exists a prime at least N with the required property. The condition `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` (i.e., 4 ∣ (p+1)) is equivalent to p ≡ -1 (mod 4) ≡ 3 (mod 4). The types (ℕ), the primality predicate (`Nat.Prime`), and the congruence match the informal claim. No hypotheses are added or dropped, so the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
